description: Warehouse hierarchy and bin management with nested trees, quantity tracking, putaway rules, and perpetual inventory GL integration.

warehouse_tree_hierarchy.description: Warehouses form a nested tree structure via parent_warehouse references. Group warehouses (is_group = true) can contain child warehouses but cannot hold stock directly. Leaf warehouses hold actual stock.

warehouse_deletion_protection.description: A warehouse cannot be deleted if it has any stock ledger entries, bin records with non-zero quantities, or child warehouses. The system must verify all dependencies before allowing deletion.

bin_projected_qty_formula.description: Projected quantity is computed as: actual_qty + ordered_qty + indented_qty + planned_qty - reserved_qty - reserved_qty_for_production - reserved_qty_for_sub_contract. This formula provides a forward-looking availability estimate.

putaway_uniqueness.description: Putaway rules must be unique per item-warehouse combination. Duplicate rules for the same item and warehouse are rejected.

putaway_capacity_validation.description: Putaway rule capacity must exceed the current stock quantity in the warehouse. The system validates that the defined capacity is achievable.

putaway_allocation_logic.description: When applying putaway rules, stock is distributed to warehouses by priority (lowest number first), then by available capacity. If the highest-priority warehouse is full, overflow goes to the next.

perpetual_inventory_gl_link.description: Each warehouse is linked to a GL account for perpetual inventory. Stock value changes in the bin are reflected as GL entries in the linked account.

given: user provides a warehouse name and company
warehouse name is unique within the company

then.description: New warehouse created in the hierarchy

create_warehouse.result: Warehouse created and available for stock transactions

given: a stock transaction is processed affecting an item-warehouse combination

then.description: Actual quantity updated from stock ledger aggregation
Projected quantity recalculated using the formula
Stock value updated based on new quantity and valuation rate

update_bin_quantities.result: Bin quantities and values reflect the latest stock position

given: incoming stock receipt has putaway rules defined for the item
at least one target warehouse has available capacity

then.description: Stock distributed across warehouses by priority and capacity

apply_putaway_rule.result: Incoming stock automatically distributed to warehouses per putaway rules

given: warehouse has no stock ledger entries
user attempts to delete or disable a warehouse
warehouse has non-zero bin quantities or stock ledger entries
warehouse_has_stock.result: Deletion blocked because warehouse still contains stock

given: user attempts to delete a group warehouse
warehouse_has_children.result: Deletion blocked because warehouse has child nodes

disable_warehouse.result: Warehouse disabled and excluded from future transactions

errors.message: Cannot delete warehouse: it contains stock or has ledger entries.
Cannot delete warehouse: it has child warehouses in the hierarchy.
Warehouse does not belong to the specified company.
A putaway rule already exists for this item-warehouse combination.
Putaway capacity must be greater than the current stock in the warehouse.

events.name: warehouse.created
events.description: New warehouse created in the hierarchy

events.name: warehouse.disabled
events.description: Warehouse disabled and excluded from transactions

events.name: bin.qty_changed
events.description: Bin quantities updated after a stock transaction

events.name: putaway.applied
events.description: Putaway rules applied to distribute incoming stock

related.reason: Stock entries are the primary source of bin quantity changes
Serial and batch tracking integrates with warehouse stock positions

goals.description: Warehouse hierarchy and bin management with nested trees, quantity tracking, putaway rules, and perpetual inventory GL integration.

success_metrics.measurement: Successful operations divided by total attempts
Failed operations divided by total attempts

tradeoffs.reason: inventory counts must be precise to prevent stockouts and overstock
